post.description:
Create a workflow run.

Two creation paths, discriminated by ``restart_of``:

- ``restart_of`` is ``None``: fresh run. ``workflow_id`` is required;
  ``documents``, ``json_inputs``, and ``version`` carry the run inputs.
- ``restart_of`` is set: restart of an existing run. The source run's
  inputs are inherited; ``config_source`` selects ``draft`` vs
  ``published`` config for the new run.

The shape of the request enforces these invariants — see
``CreateWorkflowRunRequest``.
Build CSV content for workflow run exports.

get.description: Get a single workflow run by ID.

delete.description: Delete a workflow run and its associated step data.

post.description: Cancel a pending, running, or waiting workflow run.

get.description:
List steps for a workflow run with status and artifact summaries.

Returns the canonical
``{"data": [...], "list_metadata": {"before": null, "after": null}}``
pagination envelope. Cursor pagination is not yet implemented for this
endpoint — ``list_metadata`` is always ``{before: null, after: null}``.
List reviews — the review queue, oldest first by ``created_at``.

schema.title: After

parameters.description: Cursor: only return reviews that appear after this review id in the result order. Use list_metadata.after from the previous page.

post.description:
Approve one exact review version and resume the Temporal run.

Earns its action-verb shape per the four criteria in
``meta-pattern-blueprint.md`` §2: precondition (``decision is None``),
side-effect dominates (Temporal resume signal), divergent request body vs
``/reject``, divergent response (carries ``resume_status``).

parameters.description: Opaque review id.

post.description:
Reject one exact review version and resume the Temporal run.

``reason`` is required by the request shape — "rejected without reason"
is unrepresentable on the wire.

get.description:
Deprecated. Use ``GET /workflows/artifacts/{artifact_id}`` instead.

The two-segment ``{operation}/{artifact_id}`` address violates the
flat-resource principle (a parent discriminator is encoded in the URL
when the id itself already carries enough information to disambiguate).
Retained for one release while SDK and CLI consumers migrate.

schema.title: Response Get Workflow Artifact V1 Workflows Artifacts  Operation   Artifact Id  Get

get.description:
List artifacts produced by a workflow run.

Returns the canonical
``{"data": [...], "list_metadata": {"before": null, "after": null}}``
pagination envelope. Each ``data`` item is a flattened
``WorkflowArtifact`` record (operation-tagged dereferenced artifact);
arbitrary operation-specific fields are preserved. Cursor pagination is
not yet implemented — ``list_metadata`` is always
``{before: null, after: null}``.

Filters: provide either ``run_id`` (list all artifacts in a run) or
``step_id`` (single-step lookup). When both are absent the request is
rejected with 400.

parameters.name: run_id

schema.description: Workflow run ID whose artifacts should be listed. Required unless ``step_id`` is provided.
schema.title: Run Id

parameters.description: Workflow run ID whose artifacts should be listed. Required unless ``step_id`` is provided.

parameters.name: operation

schema.description: Optional artifact operation filter
schema.title: Operation

parameters.description: Optional artifact operation filter

parameters.name: block_id

schema.description: Optional block_id or step_id filter
schema.title: Block Id

parameters.description: Optional block_id or step_id filter

parameters.name: step_id

schema.description: Optional step id filter. When provided, returns the single artifact attached to that step (or an empty list if the step has no artifact). ``run_id`` is not required when ``step_id`` is set — it is resolved from the step record.
schema.title: Step Id

parameters.description: Optional step id filter. When provided, returns the single artifact attached to that step (or an empty list if the step has no artifact). ``run_id`` is not required when ``step_id`` is set — it is resolved from the step record.

post.description:
Create a workflow-scoped test run.

Scoping identity comes from the body — ``workflow_id`` and/or
``test_id`` per ``CreateWorkflowTestRunRequest``. The
``?workflow_id=…`` query parameter is the legacy entry-point and is
deprecated; callers should migrate to the body field.

parameters.name: workflow_id

schema.description: DEPRECATED — pass ``workflow_id`` in the request body instead. Retained for one release so existing SDK/CLI callers continue to work; the body field takes precedence when both are set.

get.description:
List all blocks for a workflow.

Returns all blocks in the workflow draft wrapped in the canonical
``{"data": [...], "list_metadata": {"before": null, "after": null}}``
pagination envelope. ID pagination is not yet implemented for this
endpoint — ``list_metadata`` is always ``{before: null, after: null}``.

post.description:
Create a new block in a workflow.

This creates a block in the live workflow_blocks collection.

get.description: Get a single block by ID.

patch.description:
Update a block with partial data.

Only the provided fields are updated. This enables targeted updates
like position changes without affecting other block properties.

delete.description:
Delete a block from a workflow.

This also deletes any edges connected to this block.

get.description:
List all edges for a workflow.

Optionally filter by source or target block ID. Returns the canonical
``{"data": [...], "list_metadata": {"before": null, "after": null}}``
pagination envelope. ID pagination is not yet implemented for this
endpoint — ``list_metadata`` is always ``{before: null, after: null}``.

Create a new edge connecting two blocks.

Validates that:
- Both source and target blocks exist in the workflow
- The connection is semantically valid (type compatibility, container rules, etc.)

get.description: Get a single edge by ID.

delete.description: Delete an edge from a workflow.

post.description:
Create an experiment run.

The ``experiment_id`` and (optionally) ``workflow_id`` live in the
body — flat-resource shape per meta-pattern-blueprint §1. When
``workflow_id`` is absent the experiment's stored workflow is used;
when present it must match (the validation rejects mismatched pairs
with 404, defending against confused-deputy callers).

schema.title: Response Get Experiment Run Metrics V1 Workflows Experiments Runs  Run Id  Metrics Get

post.description:
Create an experiment.

When ``source_experiment_id`` is set, duplicates the source experiment
(block, name + "(Copy)", n_consensus, documents) and rejects any other
field. Otherwise creates a fresh experiment from the provided fields.

Filtered list of steps joined with fingerprint.

Response is a JSON array (no paginated wrapper) — matches the
locked ``StepQueryResult[]`` contract on the frontend.

post.description:
Create a new workflow.

The workflow starts unpublished and is scaffolded with a default
"Document" input block in the live block collection.

get.description:
Get a single workflow by ID.

Returns workflow metadata only.
Use GET /{workflow_id}/entities for the live draft graph stored in
separate block and edge collections.

delete.description:
Delete a workflow and all its associated entities.

This deletes:
- The workflow document
- All blocks and edges (live collections)
- All block and edge snapshots
- All workflow snapshots

post.description:
Diagnose a workflow graph payload for the given workflow.

When the request omits ``blocks`` and ``edges`` (both ``None``) the route
loads the persisted draft from MongoDB. When either is provided (including
an explicit empty list) the request body is diagnosed as-is.

Validate declarative YAML without mutating workflow state.

Contract:
- parse/compile failures return `is_valid=False` with structured issues
- warnings do not make the document invalid
- counts reflect the canonical compiled graph, not raw authored block count

Compute the declarative reconcile plan against the current draft workflow.

Contract:
- plan compares authored YAML against current draft state
- canonical exported YAML should plan as `noop` against the same draft

Apply declarative YAML to draft workflow state.

Contract:
- apply writes canonical draft state, not authored formatting
- re-applying canonical exported YAML against unchanged draft state should
  return an empty resource_changes list

get.description: Export draft workflow state as canonical declarative YAML.

post.description:
Publish a workflow.

This creates an immutable snapshot of the workflow configuration, making it available for workflow runs.
The live entities remain unchanged so users can continue editing.

Create a new asynchronous job.

The job will be queued for processing and can be polled for status
using the retrieve endpoint.

Actor.title: Actor
Actor.description: One actor shape for humans, agents, and models.

api_key_id.title: Api Key Id
api_key_id.description: API key id used to start the run, when known

ApiTrigger.title: ApiTrigger
ApiTrigger.description: Run started programmatically via the public API.

version_id.title: Version Id
version_id.description: Exact content-addressed key of the version to approve.

AssertionSchemaDep.title: AssertionSchemaDep
AssertionSchemaDep.description: Single-rule schema dependency for Level 2 drift detection.

AssertionSpec-Input.title: AssertionSpec
AssertionSpec-Input.description:
Block-test assertion against one declared output handle.

``target`` is the only supported shape: an output handle id and an
optional relative path inside that handle's payload.

AssertionSpec-Output.title: AssertionSpec
AssertionSpec-Output.description:
Block-test assertion against one declared output handle.

``target`` is the only supported shape: an output handle id and an
optional relative path inside that handle's payload.

waiting_for_block_ids.description: Block IDs that are waiting for review

AwaitingReviewRun.title: AwaitingReviewRun
AwaitingReviewRun.description: The run is paused on at least one gated block.

left.title: Left
left.description: Left coordinate of the bounding box, relative to page width (0.0 = left edge, 1.0 = right edge).

top.title: Top
top.description: Top coordinate of the bounding box, relative to page height (0.0 = top edge, 1.0 = bottom edge).

width.title: Width
width.description: Width of the bounding box, relative to page width (0.0–1.0).

height.title: Height
height.description: Height of the bounding box, relative to page height (0.0–1.0).

page.title: Page
page.description: 1-based index of the page where this field appears.

command_id.title: Command Id
command_id.description: Optional idempotency key for deduplicating cancel commands

CancelWorkflowRequest.title: CancelWorkflowRequest
CancelWorkflowRequest.description: Optional request payload for cancel workflow command idempotency.

redis_available.title: Redis Available

cancellation_status.title: Cancellation Status
cancellation_status.description: Cancellation delivery state from this request

CancelledTerminal.title: CancelledTerminal
CancelledTerminal.description: The run was cancelled before reaching a natural terminal state.

handle_key.title: Handle Key
handle_key.description: Stable machine key used by workflow classifier output handles

choices.title: Choices

likelihood.title: Likelihood

reasoning.title: Reasoning

category.title: Category

first_n_pages.description: Only use the first N pages of the document for classification. Useful for large documents where classification can be determined from early pages.

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: Free-form instructions appended to the system prompt to steer the classification.

n_consensus.title: N Consensus
n_consensus.description: Number of classification runs to use for consensus voting. Uses deterministic single-pass when set to 1.

bust_cache.title: Bust Cache
bust_cache.description: If true, skip the LLM cache and force a fresh completion

is_parallel.title: Is Parallel

CreateBlockRequest.title: CreateBlockRequest
CreateBlockRequest.description: Request to create a new block.

CreateEdgeRequest.title: CreateEdgeRequest
CreateEdgeRequest.description: Request to create a new edge.

CreateExperimentRequest.title: CreateExperimentRequest
CreateExperimentRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/workflows/experiments.

Two modes:

- **Create from scratch** — provide ``block_id``, ``name``, optional
  ``document_captures``/``documents``/``n_consensus``. ``source_experiment_id``
  is omitted.
- **Duplicate an existing experiment** — provide only
  ``source_experiment_id``. The handler copies the source's block, name
  (with ``(Copy)`` suffix), n_consensus, and documents. All other fields
  must be omitted.

The discriminator is whether ``source_experiment_id`` is set. This is
validated server-side: combining a source with any other field is
rejected so the two modes stay structurally distinct.

experiment_id.title: Experiment Id
experiment_id.description: The experiment to create a run for.

workflow_id.title: Workflow Id
workflow_id.description: Optional. When omitted, the workflow is derived from the experiment record. When supplied, must match the experiment's workflow_id (404 otherwise).

CreateExperimentRunRequest.title: CreateExperimentRunRequest
CreateExperimentRunRequest.description:
Body for the flat ``POST /workflows/experiments/runs`` route.

Carries the scoping identity in the body — no parent id in the URL
(meta-pattern-blueprint §1). ``workflow_id`` is optional because it is
derivable from the experiment record; when supplied it is validated
against the experiment's stored workflow.

json_schema.title: Json Schema
json_schema.description: JSON schema describing the structured output

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use for the extraction

image_resolution_dpi.title: Image Resolution Dpi
image_resolution_dpi.description: Resolution of the image sent to the LLM

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: Free-form instructions appended to the system prompt to steer the extraction.

n_consensus.title: N Consensus
n_consensus.description: Number of consensus extraction runs to perform. Uses deterministic single-pass when set to 1.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: User-defined metadata to associate with this extraction

additional_messages.title: Additional Messages
additional_messages.description: Additional chat messages forwarded to the extraction model.

bust_cache.title: Bust Cache
bust_cache.description: If true, skip the LLM cache and force a fresh completion

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Max 16 pairs; keys ≤64 chars, values ≤512 chars

snapshot.title: Snapshot
snapshot.description: The full reviewed snapshot to store as an immutable version. The object must match the gated block type: extract uses the raw output object; classifier uses {'category': string}; split uses {'documents': [{'name': string, 'pages': positive sorted int[]}]}; for_each uses {'partitions': [{'key': string, 'pages': positive sorted int[]}]}. The server validates the shape and stores the exact submitted object when valid.

CreateReviewVersionRequest.title: CreateReviewVersionRequest
CreateReviewVersionRequest.description:
POST /workflows/reviews/versions body.

Public callers create corrections, not seeds. ``parent_id`` must
reference an existing version under the same ``review_id``; seed versions
are created by the runtime through the store path.

subdocuments.title: Subdocuments
subdocuments.description: The subdocuments to split the document into

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to use to split the document

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: Free-form instructions appended to the system prompt to steer the split.

n_consensus.title: N Consensus
n_consensus.description: Number of consensus split runs to perform. Uses deterministic single-pass when set to 1.

bust_cache.title: Bust Cache
bust_cache.description: If true, skip the LLM cache and force a fresh completion

uploadHeaders.title: Uploadheaders
uploadHeaders.description: Headers required by the signed upload URL

workflow_id.title: Workflow Id
workflow_id.description: Required when starting a fresh run. Optional when ``restart_of`` is set (inferred from the source run).

restart_of.title: Restart Of
restart_of.description: When present, the new run is created as a restart of this source run id (the source run's inputs are inherited).

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Mapping of start-document block IDs to their input documents. Only valid for fresh-run creation (``restart_of`` is None).

json_inputs.title: Json Inputs
json_inputs.description: Mapping of start-json block IDs to their input JSON data. Only valid for fresh-run creation (``restart_of`` is None).

version.title: Version
version.description: Workflow version to run: 'production', 'draft', or a pinned version id like 'ver_...'. Only valid for fresh-run creation.

config_source.title: Config Source
config_source.description: Required when ``restart_of`` is set. Config source for the restarted run.

command_id.title: Command Id
command_id.description: Optional idempotency key for deduplicating restart commands. Only valid when ``restart_of`` is set.

CreateWorkflowRunRequest.title: CreateWorkflowRunRequest
CreateWorkflowRunRequest.description:
Flat CRUD-style request body for ``POST /v1/workflows/runs``.

Handles two creation flows by discriminating on ``restart_of``:

- **Fresh run** (``restart_of is None``): requires ``workflow_id``; accepts
  ``documents``, ``json_inputs``, and ``version``.
- **Restart** (``restart_of`` set): re-runs an existing run by inheriting
  its inputs. ``workflow_id`` is optional (inferred from the source run);
  ``documents``/``json_inputs``/``version`` are not accepted and the
  caller must supply ``config_source`` (``draft`` or ``published``).

Shape-enforced invariants — fresh-vs-restart bodies are distinguishable
via the schema, not prose.

CreateWorkflowTestRunRequest.title: CreateWorkflowTestRunRequest
CreateWorkflowTestRunRequest.description:
Body shape for ``POST /v1/workflows/tests/runs``.

Carries the scoping identity (``workflow_id`` or ``test_id``) in the
body — no parent id in the URL (meta-pattern-blueprint §1). At least
one of (body.``workflow_id``, body.``test_id``, query.``workflow_id``)
must be present at request time; the at-least-one check lives on the
route so legacy callers passing ``workflow_id`` via the deprecated
query param continue to work without putting it in the body too.

Decision.title: Decision
Decision.description: The one terminal decision recorded against one exact :class:`ReviewVersion`.

rendered_plan.title: Rendered Plan
rendered_plan.default: No changes. Workflow spec is up to date.

rendered_plan.title: Rendered Plan
rendered_plan.default: No changes. Workflow spec is up to date.

file.description: Information about the source file (input document or template PDF).

model.title: Model

instructions.description: Instructions supplied with the edit request.

template_id.description: Template id used when the edit was created from a template; null for direct-document edits.

form_data.description: Filled form fields (positions, descriptions, and filled values).

filled_document.description: PDF with the filled form values rendered in.

file.description: File information for the empty PDF template.

form_fields.title: Form Fields

Run-level summary plus block-specific diagnostics.

``prior_run_id`` + ``prior_score`` populate when the request opts into

PublishWorkflowRequest.description: Optional request body for publishing a workflow.

parent_run_id.title: Parent Run Id
parent_run_id.description: ID of the parent run that was restarted

RestartTrigger.title: RestartTrigger
RestartTrigger.description: Run created by restarting a parent run.

ReviewAllOf.title: ReviewAllOf
ReviewAllOf.description: Gate fires only if ALL child predicates fire.

ReviewAnyOf.title: ReviewAnyOf
ReviewAnyOf.description:
Gate fires if ANY child predicate fires. Evaluated in list order;
`triggered_by` reports the first match (decision: first-match wins).

ReviewAnyRequiredFieldNull.title: ReviewAnyRequiredFieldNull
ReviewAnyRequiredFieldNull.description:
Gate when any required field in the extract schema is null/missing.

First-class predicate because this is the most common real-world driver
for structured-extraction review.

ReviewAnySplitPagesLt.title: ReviewAnySplitPagesLt
ReviewAnySplitPagesLt.description: Gate when any resulting split has fewer than `min_pages` pages.

ReviewBoundaryConfidenceLt.title: ReviewBoundaryConfidenceLt
ReviewBoundaryConfidenceLt.description: Gate when any split boundary's confidence is below `threshold`.

ReviewBranchIn.title: ReviewBranchIn
ReviewBranchIn.description:
Gate when a conditional-style result chose a branch in `branches`.

Conditional blocks are intentionally not reviewable. The predicate remains in
the global union so old review/evaluator payloads can still be parsed.

ReviewCategoryIn.title: ReviewCategoryIn
ReviewCategoryIn.description: Gate when the predicted category is in `categories` (e.g., review fraud alerts).

ReviewConfidenceLt.title: ReviewConfidenceLt
ReviewConfidenceLt.description:
Gate if the overall block confidence is below `threshold`.

Note: LLM confidences are poorly calibrated; per-field confidence
(ReviewFieldConfidenceLt) tends to behave better.

ReviewFieldConfidenceLt.title: ReviewFieldConfidenceLt
ReviewFieldConfidenceLt.description: Gate when the field at `path` has confidence below `threshold`.

ReviewJsonCondition.title: ReviewJsonCondition
ReviewJsonCondition.description:
Gate when a conditional-block-style JSON condition matches.

The `condition` payload uses the same shape as workflow conditional blocks:
one condition group with `sub_conditions` and a `logical_operator`. Paths are
evaluated against a review condition root, for example `data.invoice_total`
or `likelihoods.invoice_total`.

ReviewResponse.title: ReviewResponse
ReviewResponse.description: Public API shape for one review. Strips internal fields.

ReviewSplitCountNeq.title: ReviewSplitCountNeq
ReviewSplitCountNeq.description: Gate when the number of resulting splits != `expected`.

ReviewSummaryResponse.title: ReviewSummaryResponse
ReviewSummaryResponse.description: Public API shape for one review queue row.

ReviewTopMarginLt.title: ReviewTopMarginLt
ReviewTopMarginLt.description: Gate when (top1_prob - top2_prob) < `margin` — model was torn.

ReviewValidationFailed.title: ReviewValidationFailed
ReviewValidationFailed.description: Gate if the block output fails its declared schema validation.

ReviewVersionResponse.title: ReviewVersionResponse
ReviewVersionResponse.description: Public API shape for one immutable review version.

documents.title: Documents

json_data.description: start-json block ID -> input JSON data

Mirrors ``StepQueryResult`` in

handle_outputs.title: Handle Outputs

StepsQueryRequest.title: StepsQueryRequest
StepsQueryRequest.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/workflows/steps/query``.

Filters mirror the indexed columns on ``step_fingerprints`` so the
``$match`` stage stays index-friendly. Status is the one filter that
targets the joined ``workflow_run_steps`` document (applied as a
post-``$lookup`` ``$match`` on ``lifecycle.status``).

allowed_senders.title: Allowed Senders

edges.title: Edges

re_propagate.title: Re Propagate
re_propagate.description: Recompute derived schemas before validating the graph

WorkflowGraphDiagnosisRequest.title: WorkflowGraphDiagnosisRequest
WorkflowGraphDiagnosisRequest.description:
Request payload for ``POST /workflows/{id}/diagnose-graph``.

``blocks`` and ``edges`` are both nullable so the route can tell apart
two intents:

* ``blocks`` / ``edges`` omitted (``None``): diagnose the persisted draft
  — the route loads blocks and edges from MongoDB.
* ``blocks`` / ``edges`` provided (even as ``[]``): diagnose the in-memory
  graph as-is — used by editor previews and ``workflows spec validate``
  against a stub file.

Sending only one side (e.g. ``blocks=[]`` without ``edges``) is treated as
explicit-empty for the missing side too; the persisted-draft fallback only
kicks in when both are omitted.

published_at.title: Published At
published_at.description: When the workflow was last published

ReviewDecisionResponse.title: ReviewDecisionResponse

The shape carries ``resume_status`` so callers can see whether the
Temporal resume signal succeeded — meta-pattern §2 action-endpoint
criterion #4 (divergent response shape).
